MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom:
Atty. John J. Roach, Esq. of Law Office of John J. Roach (Tel. 415.851.4557) for plaintiff Man Kit Lam;
Atty. Alex Lemberg, Esq. (Tel. 415.921.9300) for non-party deponent Brandee Marckmann.

OTHER BRANDEE MARCKMANN'S Motion For Order Quashing Portions Of Deposition Subpoena And Staying Taking Of Deposition And For Sanctions.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for parties to the motion (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their bealf.

Matter called, argument is held.

At the conclusion of argument, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows: the motion is denied without prejudice. There was no separate statement filed as required by California Rules of Court 3.3145. No sanctions.

Mr. Roach is to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge Pro Tem: Tom Cohen; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Matter not reported. (JPT/302)

Off Calendar Aug-03-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
John J. Roach, Esq. (415.851.4557; john@representmyinjury.com) for Plaintiff, MAN KIT LAM.
Alex Lamberg, Esq. (415.921.9300; alex@alexlemberg.com) for moving party/non-party/deponent, MARGARET KELMAN.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

OTHER MARGARET KELMAN's Motion For Order Quashing Subpoena And Staying Taking Of Deposition And For Sanctions:

Non-party deponent Margaret Zoe Kelman's Motion For Order Quashing Deposition Subpoena And Staying Taking Of Deposition And For Sanctions is GRANTED and DENIED as follows. The Motion to Quash the deposition is DENIED. See Reply, 2:10-11 "Ms. Kelman was indeed photographed at Clement Street and 3rd Avenue in San Francisco on April 11, 2021, and was present at that time." Non-party Kelman shall appear for deposition within two weeks of notice of this order. The Motion to Quash the document requests is GRANTED as to requests 2, 3, 4, 5, and 7. The Motion to Quash the document requests is DENIED as to requests 1, 6, and 8. All requests for sanctions are denied.

Moving party to submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312 via email to the judge pro tem at Peter.Catalanotti@wilsonelser.com by noon today.

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Catalanotti, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
